title: Post-workout Light Pungent Irish Branston-pickle Bowl
description: Refreshing post-workout meal from Ireland, featuring the tangy flavor of Branston pickle, with a sticky soy-based lacquer glaze and a protein-rich egg-based sauce. Perfect for those who need a light yet satisfying meal after exercise.

ingredients.name: Boiled new potatoes
ingredients.notes: Choose potatoes with a firm texture, for the best results.

ingredients.name: Branston pickle
ingredients.notes: This recipe uses the original version of Branston pickle, but you can use low-sodium or homemade alternatives as well.

ingredients.name: Lacquer glaze
ingredients.notes: Mix equal parts soy sauce and rice vinegar for the best results.

ingredients.name: Smoked salmon
ingredients.notes: Choose high-quality, smoked salmon for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Fresh parsley adds a burst of freshness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Egg-based sauce
ingredients.notes: See instructions for making the egg-based sauce.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a richness to the dish.

instructions: Boil the new potatoes in salted water until they are tender, for about 10 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and set them aside to cool down.
In a small pan, heat the lacquer glaze over medium heat until it thickens slightly.
Add the smoked salmon to the pan and cook for about 2 minutes, until it starts to flake apart.
Transfer the salmon to a plate and set it aside to cool down.
In a separate pan, heat the egg-based sauce over medium heat until it thickens slightly.
Add the chopped parsley to the pan and stir to combine.
Season the sauce with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika.
Add the cooled-down potatoes to the pan and stir to combine.
Transfer the potatoes to a serving bowl and top them with the smoked salmon, egg-based sauce, and a sprinkle of fresh dill.
If using butter, melt it in a small pan over low heat and drizzle it over the dish.
Garnish the dish with a sprinkle of lemon juice and a few slices of fresh dill.
